test_cases.description: Verifies that `web/.nvmrc` exists in the repository and contains exactly the string `22` to pin Node.js 22 LTS.
preconditions: The repository has been cloned to a local working directory.
The `web/` directory has been initialized per the scaffold spec.
test_cases.expected: File exists and its trimmed content equals `22`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/.nvmrc').trim()
assert content == '22'

test_cases.description: Verifies that `web/package-lock.json` is present in the repository and is consistent with `web/package.json` so that `npm ci` produces a reproducible install.
test_cases.expected: `web/package-lock.json` exists as a non-empty file; running `npm ci` in `web/` exits with code 0.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert fileExists('web/package-lock.json')
assert fileSize('web/package-lock.json') > 0
exitCode = runCommand('npm ci', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that all version strings in `package.json` `dependencies` and `devDependencies` use the semver caret (`^`) prefix.
test_cases.expected: Every value in both `dependencies` and `devDependencies` maps starts with `^`.

test_cases.description: Verifies that the root `.gitignore` contains entries for `web/dist/` and `web/node_modules/` so those paths are excluded from git tracking.
preconditions: The root `.gitignore` file exists at the repository root.
test_cases.expected: `git check-ignore web/dist/ web/node_modules/` returns both paths as ignored (exit code 0 for each).
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result1 = runCommand('git check-ignore web/dist/', cwd=repoRoot)
assert result1.exitCode == 0
result2 = runCommand('git check-ignore web/node_modules/', cwd=repoRoot)
assert result2.exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that there is no `web/.gitignore` file created by this spec, and the only web-specific ignore rules are the two entries in the root `.gitignore`.
preconditions: Repository has been initialized per the scaffold spec.
test_cases.expected: `web/.gitignore` does not exist.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert NOT fileExists('web/.gitignore')

test_cases.description: Verifies that all required top-level files are committed under `web/`.
preconditions: Repository has been cloned and the scaffold has been applied.
test_cases.expected: All of `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.node.json`, `vite.config.ts`, `tailwind.config.ts`, `postcss.config.js`, `eslint.config.js`, `components.json`, `index.html`, `.nvmrc`, and `package-lock.json` exist under `web/`.

test_cases.description: Verifies that all required source files and directories exist under `web/src/`.
preconditions: Repository has been cloned and the scaffold has been applied.
test_cases.expected: Files `main.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `globals.css`, `vite-env.d.ts`, `lib/utils.ts`, `routes/index.tsx`, `routes/not-found.tsx`, and `components/ui/button.tsx` all exist under `web/src/`.

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/main.tsx` mounts `<App />` into the DOM element with id `root` using React 18's `createRoot` API.
test_cases.expected: The file contains a call to `createRoot` targeting an element with id `root`, and calls `.render(<App />)` or equivalent.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/main.tsx')
assert content.contains('createRoot')
assert content.contains('root')
assert content.contains('App')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `index.html` is at `web/index.html` (not inside `src/`) and references `src/main.tsx` as the module entry point.
test_cases.expected: File exists at `web/index.html` (not `web/src/index.html`), and its content contains a `<script type="module" src="/src/main.tsx">` tag or equivalent.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert fileExists('web/index.html')
assert NOT fileExists('web/src/index.html')
content = readFile('web/index.html')
assert content.contains('src/main.tsx')
assert content.contains('type="module"')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `web/components.json` exists, is valid JSON, and is parseable by the shadcn/ui CLI.
preconditions: Repository has been cloned and `npx shadcn-ui init` has been run.
test_cases.expected: `web/components.json` exists and can be parsed as valid JSON without errors.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/components.json')
parsed = parseJSON(content)
assert parsed != null
assert typeof(parsed) == 'object'

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/lib/utils.ts` exports a `cn()` function that is callable.
preconditions: Dependencies have been installed via `npm ci`.
test_cases.expected: The file contains and exports a function named `cn`; TypeScript resolves `import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'` without error.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/lib/utils.ts')
assert content.contains('export') AND content.contains('cn')
exitCode = runCommand('tsc --noEmit', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `package.json` declares all four required runtime dependencies under `dependencies`.
test_cases.expected: `dependencies` contains keys `react`, `react-dom`, `react-router-dom`, and `@tanstack/react-query`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = parseJSON(readFile('web/package.json'))
required = ['react', 'react-dom', 'react-router-dom', '@tanstack/react-query']
for dep in required:
  assert dep in pkg.dependencies

test_cases.description: Verifies that `package.json` declares all required dev dependencies under `devDependencies`.
test_cases.expected: `devDependencies` contains all required packages including `typescript`, `vite`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `tailwindcss`, `postcss`, `autoprefixer`, `eslint`, `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`, `@typescript-eslint/parser`, `eslint-plugin-react`, and `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = parseJSON(readFile('web/package.json'))
requiredDev = ['typescript','vite','@vitejs/plugin-react','tailwindcss','postcss','autoprefixer','eslint','@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin','@typescript-eslint/parser','eslint-plugin-react','eslint-plugin-react-hooks']
for dep in requiredDev:
  assert dep in pkg.devDependencies

test_cases.description: Verifies that `package.json` defines `dev`, `build`, and `lint` npm scripts.
test_cases.expected: `scripts` map contains keys `dev`, `build`, and `lint`, each with a non-empty string value.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = parseJSON(readFile('web/package.json'))
assert 'dev' in pkg.scripts AND len(pkg.scripts['dev']) > 0
assert 'build' in pkg.scripts AND len(pkg.scripts['build']) > 0
assert 'lint' in pkg.scripts AND len(pkg.scripts['lint']) > 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `tsconfig.json` enables all required compiler options and that `tsc --noEmit` exits with code 0 on a clean scaffold.
preconditions: All scaffold source files are present under `web/src/`.
test_cases.expected: `tsconfig.json` contains `strict: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`, `moduleResolution: bundler`, `target: ES2022`, `jsx: react-jsx`, `noEmit: true`, and `paths: { '@/*': ['./src/*'] }`; `tsc --noEmit` exits with code 0.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = parseJSON(readFile('web/tsconfig.json'))
co = cfg.compilerOptions
assert co.strict == true
assert co.noUncheckedIndexedAccess == true
assert co.moduleResolution == 'bundler'
assert co.target == 'ES2022'
assert co.jsx == 'react-jsx'
assert co.noEmit == true
assert co.paths['@/*'] contains './src/*'
exitCode = runCommand('tsc --noEmit', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `tsconfig.node.json` exists with required options and that `vite.config.ts` type-checks without errors when referenced by it.
test_cases.expected: `tsconfig.node.json` contains `composite: true`, `skipLibCheck: true`, `module: ESNext`, `moduleResolution: bundler`, `allowSyntheticDefaultImports: true`, and `include: ['vite.config.ts']`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = parseJSON(readFile('web/tsconfig.node.json'))
co = cfg.compilerOptions
assert co.composite == true
assert co.skipLibCheck == true
assert co.module == 'ESNext'
assert co.moduleResolution == 'bundler'
assert co.allowSyntheticDefaultImports == true
assert 'vite.config.ts' in cfg.include

test_cases.description: Verifies that `tsconfig.json` covers all TypeScript files under `web/src/` via its `include` or `rootDir` configuration.
test_cases.expected: The `include` array contains a glob covering `src/` (e.g., `src`, `src/**/*`) or `rootDir` is set to `.` / `src`, ensuring all `.tsx` and `.ts` files under `src/` are type-checked.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = parseJSON(readFile('web/tsconfig.json'))
include = cfg.include OR []
rootDir = cfg.compilerOptions.rootDir OR ''
assert (any glob in include covers 'src/') OR ('src' in rootDir)

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/vite-env.d.ts` contains an ambient declaration for `__APP_VERSION__: string`.
test_cases.expected: File contains the text `declare const __APP_VERSION__: string`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/vite-env.d.ts')
assert content.contains('declare const __APP_VERSION__: string')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `tsconfig.json` configures `compilerOptions.paths` with `@/*` mapping to `./src/*`.
test_cases.expected: `compilerOptions.paths` contains `@/*` with value `['./src/*']`; TypeScript resolves `import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'` without error.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = parseJSON(readFile('web/tsconfig.json'))
assert '@/*' in cfg.compilerOptions.paths
assert cfg.compilerOptions.paths['@/*'] == ['./src/*']
exitCode = runCommand('tsc --noEmit', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `vite.config.ts` configures `resolve.alias` mapping `@` to `./src` so Vite resolves `@/`-prefixed imports.
test_cases.expected: File contains a `resolve.alias` configuration mapping `'@'` to `path.resolve(__dirname, './src')` or equivalent; both `npm run dev` and `npm run build` resolve `@/` imports correctly.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert content.contains('resolve') AND content.contains('alias')
assert content.contains('@') AND content.contains('./src')
exitCode = runCommand('npm run build', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that all shadcn/ui component imports in the scaffold use the `@/` alias rather than relative paths.
test_cases.expected: Any import of `button.tsx` or other shadcn/ui components uses `@/components/ui/button` syntax, not relative paths like `../../components/ui/button`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/routes/index.tsx')
assert content.contains('@/components/ui/button')
assert NOT content.contains('../components/ui/button')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `web/eslint.config.js` exists and uses the ESLint 9+ flat config format (exports an array).
test_cases.expected: `eslint.config.js` exists at `web/eslint.config.js`; ESLint version is 9 or above; ESLint loads the config without errors.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert fileExists('web/eslint.config.js')
content = readFile('web/eslint.config.js')
assert content.contains('export default') OR content.contains('module.exports')
result = runCommand('eslint --version', cwd='web/')
assert result.stdout.startsWith('v9') OR versionMajor(result.stdout) >= 9
exitCode = runCommand('eslint .', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `eslint.config.js` includes TypeScript-aware linting via `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` and `@typescript-eslint/parser`.
test_cases.expected: File references `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` and `@typescript-eslint/parser`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/eslint.config.js')
assert content.contains('@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin')
assert content.contains('@typescript-eslint/parser')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `eslint.config.js` includes React-specific rules via `eslint-plugin-react` and `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`.
test_cases.expected: File references both `eslint-plugin-react` and `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/eslint.config.js')
assert content.contains('eslint-plugin-react')
assert content.contains('eslint-plugin-react-hooks')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `eslint.config.js` explicitly disables the `react/react-in-jsx-scope` rule.
test_cases.expected: File contains `'react/react-in-jsx-scope': 'off'` or equivalent; `npm run lint` produces no `react/react-in-jsx-scope` errors.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/eslint.config.js')
assert content.contains('react/react-in-jsx-scope') AND content.contains('off')
result = runCommand('npm run lint', cwd='web/')
assert NOT result.stderr.contains('react/react-in-jsx-scope')
assert result.exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `npm run lint` produces zero lint errors on the initial committed scaffold state.
preconditions: Dependencies are installed via `npm ci` in `web/`.
test_cases.expected: `npm run lint` exits with code 0 and produces no error output.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = runCommand('npm run lint', cwd='web/')
assert result.exitCode == 0
assert result.stderr.trim() == '' OR noErrorsInOutput(result.stderr)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the `lint` script in `package.json` runs both `eslint .` and `tsc --noEmit`.
test_cases.expected: The `lint` script string contains both `eslint` and `tsc --noEmit` invocations.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = parseJSON(readFile('web/package.json'))
lintScript = pkg.scripts['lint']
assert lintScript.contains('eslint')
assert lintScript.contains('tsc --noEmit')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/globals.css` includes Tailwind directives and shadcn/ui CSS variable declarations for `:root` and `.dark`.
test_cases.expected: File contains `@tailwind base`, `@tailwind components`, `@tailwind utilities`, and CSS variable declarations under `:root` and `.dark` (e.g., `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`).
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/globals.css')
assert content.contains('@tailwind base')
assert content.contains('@tailwind components')
assert content.contains('@tailwind utilities')
assert content.contains(':root')
assert content.contains('.dark')
assert content.contains('--background')
assert content.contains('--foreground')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `tailwind.config.ts` sets correct content paths, extends the theme with shadcn/ui CSS variable colour mappings, and sets `darkMode` to `'class'`.
test_cases.expected: File contains `content: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}']` or equivalent, a theme extension with `hsl(var(--background))` style colour mappings, and `darkMode: 'class'`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/tailwind.config.ts')
assert content.contains('./index.html')
assert content.contains('./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}')
assert content.contains('hsl(var(--background))')
assert content.contains("darkMode: 'class'")

test_cases.description: Verifies that `postcss.config.js` configures `tailwindcss` and `autoprefixer` as PostCSS plugins.
test_cases.expected: File references both `tailwindcss` and `autoprefixer` as plugins.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/postcss.config.js')
assert content.contains('tailwindcss')
assert content.contains('autoprefixer')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/main.tsx` imports `globals.css` to ensure global Tailwind styles are applied.
test_cases.expected: File contains an import statement for `./globals.css` or equivalent path.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/main.tsx')
assert content.contains('globals.css')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the shadcn/ui `Button` component is copied into `src/components/ui/button.tsx` and renders without CSS errors in the hello-world route.
preconditions: Vite dev server is running via `npm run dev`.
test_cases.expected: `button.tsx` exists and the page at `/` renders a Button element with Tailwind-based styles and no console CSS errors.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert fileExists('web/src/components/ui/button.tsx')
content = readFile('web/src/components/ui/button.tsx')
assert content.contains('Button')
buildExitCode = runCommand('npm run build', cwd='web/')
assert buildExitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `vite.config.ts` configures the dev server to listen on port 5173.
test_cases.expected: File contains `port: 5173` in the server configuration block.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert content.contains('port: 5173') OR content.contains('port:5173')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Vite proxy forwards `/api/*` requests to `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true` and no path rewriting.
test_cases.expected: Proxy config contains an entry for `/api` targeting `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true` and no `rewrite` property.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert content.contains('"/api"') OR content.contains("'/api'")
assert content.contains('http://localhost:8080')
assert content.contains('changeOrigin: true')
assert NOT (content.contains('rewrite') AND adjacentTo(content, '/api', 'rewrite'))

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Vite proxy forwards `/healthz` requests to `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true` and no path rewriting.
test_cases.expected: Proxy config contains an entry for `/healthz` targeting `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert content.contains('/healthz')
assert content.contains('http://localhost:8080')
assert content.contains('changeOrigin: true')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Vite proxy forwards `/readyz` requests to `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true` and no path rewriting.
test_cases.expected: Proxy config contains an entry for `/readyz` targeting `http://localhost:8080` with `changeOrigin: true`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert content.contains('/readyz')
assert content.contains('http://localhost:8080')
assert content.contains('changeOrigin: true')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `npm run dev` starts the Vite dev server on port 5173 successfully even when the Go backend at localhost:8080 is not running.
preconditions: The Go backend at `http://localhost:8080` is NOT running.
test_cases.input: Command: `npm run dev` from `web/`; nothing listening on port 8080
test_cases.expected: The Vite dev server starts and binds to port 5173 without exiting with a non-zero code; no fatal error is emitted about the missing backend.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ensureNothingOnPort(8080)
process = startBackground('npm run dev', cwd='web/')
sleep(3)
assert process.isRunning()
assert portIsListening(5173)
process.terminate()

test_cases.description: Verifies that the root `Makefile` contains a `.PHONY` target `web-dev` that runs `cd web && npm run dev`.
test_cases.expected: `Makefile` contains `web-dev` in its `.PHONY` declaration and a recipe running `cd web && npm run dev`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('Makefile')
assert content.contains('.PHONY') AND content.contains('web-dev')
assert content.contains('cd web && npm run dev')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the root `Makefile` contains a `.PHONY` target `web-build` that produces output in `web/dist/`.
test_cases.input: File: `Makefile` at repository root; command: `make web-build`
test_cases.expected: `Makefile` contains `web-build` in `.PHONY` with recipe `cd web && npm run build`; `make web-build` exits with code 0 and `web/dist/` contains built assets.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('Makefile')
assert content.contains('web-build')
assert content.contains('cd web && npm run build')
exitCode = runCommand('make web-build', cwd=repoRoot)
assert exitCode == 0
assert directoryExists('web/dist') AND directoryNotEmpty('web/dist')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the root `Makefile` contains a `.PHONY` target `web-lint` that runs ESLint and TypeScript type checking.
test_cases.input: File: `Makefile` at repository root; command: `make web-lint`
test_cases.expected: `Makefile` contains `web-lint` in `.PHONY` with recipe `cd web && npm run lint`; `make web-lint` exits with code 0.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('Makefile')
assert content.contains('web-lint')
assert content.contains('cd web && npm run lint')
exitCode = runCommand('make web-lint', cwd=repoRoot)
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that all three Makefile targets (`web-dev`, `web-build`, `web-lint`) are declared in the `.PHONY` list.
test_cases.expected: All three targets are listed in `.PHONY`; `make -n web-dev web-build web-lint` completes without errors about missing targets.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('Makefile')
phonyLine = extractLine(content, '.PHONY')
assert 'web-dev' in phonyLine OR countOccurrences(content, '.PHONY') > 1
assert 'web-build' in phonyLine
assert 'web-lint' in phonyLine
exitCode = runCommand('make -n web-dev web-build web-lint', cwd=repoRoot)
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/routes/index.tsx` renders a heading containing the text `af-hub`.
test_cases.expected: File contains a JSX heading element (e.g., `<h1>`) with the text `af-hub`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/routes/index.tsx')
assert content.contains('af-hub')
assert content.contains('<h1>') OR content.contains('<h2>') OR content.contains('<h')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/routes/index.tsx` displays the application version using the `__APP_VERSION__` constant injected by Vite.
test_cases.expected: File references `__APP_VERSION__` in its JSX output; TypeScript does not error on this reference.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/routes/index.tsx')
assert content.contains('__APP_VERSION__')
exitCode = runCommand('tsc --noEmit', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/routes/index.tsx` renders at least one shadcn/ui `Button` component imported via the `@/` alias.
test_cases.expected: File imports `Button` from `@/components/ui/button` and renders `<Button>` in its JSX.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/routes/index.tsx')
assert content.contains("from '@/components/ui/button'")
assert content.contains('<Button')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `vite.config.ts` defines `__APP_VERSION__` via the `define` option.
test_cases.expected: File contains a `define` section with `__APP_VERSION__` set to `JSON.stringify(process.env.npm_package_version)`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert content.contains('define')
assert content.contains('__APP_VERSION__')
assert content.contains('JSON.stringify(process.env.npm_package_version)')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/App.tsx` configures React Router and registers the hello-world route at `/`.
test_cases.expected: File sets up `BrowserRouter` or `createBrowserRouter` and registers `src/routes/index.tsx` at the path `/`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/App.tsx')
assert content.contains('BrowserRouter') OR content.contains('createBrowserRouter') OR content.contains('RouterProvider')
assert content.contains('routes/index') OR content.contains('IndexPage') OR content.contains('path="/"')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/App.tsx` wraps the router with `QueryClientProvider` from `@tanstack/react-query`.
test_cases.expected: File imports `QueryClientProvider` and `QueryClient` from `@tanstack/react-query` and wraps the router with `<QueryClientProvider client={...}>`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/App.tsx')
assert content.contains('QueryClientProvider')
assert content.contains('@tanstack/react-query')
assert content.contains('QueryClient')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/App.tsx` registers a wildcard catch-all route that renders `src/routes/not-found.tsx`.
test_cases.expected: File contains a route with `path="*"` or equivalent wildcard that renders the not-found component.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/App.tsx')
assert content.contains('path="*"') OR content.contains("path: '*'")
assert content.contains('not-found') OR content.contains('NotFound')

test_cases.description: Verifies that `src/routes/not-found.tsx` displays a human-readable 404 message and a link back to `/`.
test_cases.expected: File contains text like `404` and `Page not found`, plus a `<Link to="/">` or `<a href="/">` element navigating back to `/`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/routes/not-found.tsx')
assert content.contains('404')
assert content.contains('not found') OR content.contains('Not Found')
assert content.contains('to="/"') OR content.contains("to='/'")

property_tests.description: For any committed state of the `web/` scaffold, `npm run lint` (running `eslint .` and `tsc --noEmit`) must always exit with code 0 — no ESLint errors and no TypeScript type errors.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any combination of scaffold files in `web/src/` as committed (no mutations); the property is checked against the exact committed scaffold without modifications.
property_tests.invariant_check:
exitCode = runCommand('npm run lint', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode == 0
# Also verify individually:
eslintCode = runCommand('eslint .', cwd='web/')
assert eslintCode == 0
tscCode = runCommand('tsc --noEmit', cwd='web/')
assert tscCode == 0

property_tests.description: For any `@/`-prefixed import in any file under `web/src/`, the import resolves consistently in both `tsc --noEmit` (TypeScript paths) and the Vite bundler (resolve.alias), with no discrepancy.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any valid TypeScript/TSX file added under `web/src/` that uses an `@/`-prefixed import (e.g., `@/lib/utils`, `@/components/ui/button`).
property_tests.invariant_check:
# Both tools must succeed
tscCode = runCommand('tsc --noEmit', cwd='web/')
assert tscCode == 0
buildCode = runCommand('npm run build', cwd='web/')
assert buildCode == 0
# Config consistency check
tscCfg = parseJSON(readFile('web/tsconfig.json'))
assert '@/*' in tscCfg.compilerOptions.paths
viteCfg = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert viteCfg.contains('alias') AND viteCfg.contains('@') AND viteCfg.contains('./src')

property_tests.description: For any HTTP request whose path begins with `/api`, or exactly matches `/healthz` or `/readyz`, the path forwarded to `http://localhost:8080` must be identical to the original request path with no segments stripped or rewritten.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any request path beginning with `/api` (e.g., `/api/users`, `/api/v1/foo/bar`) or exactly `/healthz` or `/readyz`.
property_tests.invariant_check:
# Structural check: no rewrite keys in proxy config
proxyConfig = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
for path in ['/api', '/healthz', '/readyz']:
  proxyEntry = extractProxyEntry(proxyConfig, path)
  assert proxyEntry.contains('changeOrigin: true')
  assert NOT proxyEntry.contains('rewrite')
  assert proxyEntry.contains('http://localhost:8080')
# No path rewriting means forwarded path == original path

property_tests.description: For any execution of `npx shadcn-ui add <component>` in the `web/` directory after the scaffold is committed, the command succeeds without requiring re-initialization because `components.json` is present and consistent.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any valid shadcn/ui component name (e.g., `button`, `input`, `dialog`) passed to `npx shadcn-ui add`.
property_tests.invariant_check:
assert fileExists('web/components.json')
parsed = parseJSON(readFile('web/components.json'))
assert parsed != null
# Verify alias in components.json matches tsconfig and vite config
assert parsed.aliases.utils == '@/lib/utils' OR parsed.aliases contains '@/'
tscCfg = parseJSON(readFile('web/tsconfig.json'))
assert '@/*' in tscCfg.compilerOptions.paths
viteCfg = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert viteCfg.contains('@') AND viteCfg.contains('./src')

property_tests.description: For any execution of `npm ci && npm run build` in the `web/` directory on any machine, the build succeeds and produces output in `web/dist/` using exact dependency versions from `package-lock.json`.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any clean environment (fresh clone, no existing `node_modules/`) where `npm ci` is run before `npm run build`.
property_tests.invariant_check:
# Simulate clean install
removeDirectory('web/node_modules')
exitCode1 = runCommand('npm ci', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode1 == 0
exitCode2 = runCommand('npm run build', cwd='web/')
assert exitCode2 == 0
assert directoryExists('web/dist') AND directoryNotEmpty('web/dist')
# Version injection
buildAssets = firstJSFile('web/dist/')
# __APP_VERSION__ is defined (even if 'undefined' when npm_package_version is unset)
assert buildAssets != null

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that the setup is considered incomplete when `web/.nvmrc` is absent or contains a version other than `22`.
preconditions: A test environment where `web/.nvmrc` can be modified or removed.
edge_case_tests.input: `web/.nvmrc` is either absent or contains a string other than `22` (e.g., `18` or `20`).
edge_case_tests.expected: The verification check for `web/.nvmrc` fails: the file is either missing or contains an incorrect version string.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
# Case 1: file absent
removeFile('web/.nvmrc')
assert NOT fileExists('web/.nvmrc')

# Case 2: wrong version
writeFile('web/.nvmrc', '18')
content = readFile('web/.nvmrc').trim()
assert content != '22'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when `components.json` is absent, future `npx shadcn-ui add <component>` invocations fail or prompt for re-initialization.
preconditions: `web/` directory is initialized but `components.json` has been removed.
edge_case_tests.input: `web/components.json` is deleted; `npx shadcn-ui add button` is run from `web/`.
edge_case_tests.expected: The command exits with a non-zero code or outputs a message requesting re-initialization; it does not silently succeed.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
removeFile('web/components.json')
assert NOT fileExists('web/components.json')
result = runCommand('npx shadcn-ui add button', cwd='web/')
assert result.exitCode != 0 OR result.stdout.contains('init') OR result.stderr.contains('init')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when `src/lib/utils.ts` is absent, all shadcn/ui component copies fail to compile due to unresolved `cn()` imports.
edge_case_tests.expected: `tsc --noEmit` exits with a non-zero code reporting a module-not-found error for `@/lib/utils`.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
removeFile('web/src/lib/utils.ts')
assert NOT fileExists('web/src/lib/utils.ts')
result = runCommand('tsc --noEmit', cwd='web/')
assert result.exitCode != 0
assert result.stderr.contains('utils') OR result.stderr.contains('Cannot find module')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that if `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` is omitted or set to `false`, unsafe array/object index access goes undetected.
preconditions: `web/tsconfig.json` exists and can be modified for this test.
edge_case_tests.input: `tsconfig.json` has `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` set to `false` or omitted; a source file accesses an array index without checking for `undefined`.
edge_case_tests.expected: The scaffold's `tsconfig.json` must have `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`; any deviation must be detectable by reading the config.

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when `@/` alias is configured in `tsconfig.json` but missing from `vite.config.ts`, the Vite bundler fails to resolve `@/`-prefixed imports.
preconditions: A test copy of `vite.config.ts` with the `resolve.alias` section removed.
edge_case_tests.input: `vite.config.ts` has no `resolve.alias` mapping for `@`; `npm run build` is run.
edge_case_tests.expected: `npm run build` exits with a non-zero code reporting a module-not-found error for paths beginning with `@/`.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
# Verify alias is present in vite.config.ts
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert content.contains('alias')
assert content.contains('@')
assert content.contains('./src')

# Simulate missing alias: build should fail
modifiedContent = removeAliasSection(content)
writeFile('web/vite.config.ts.bak', content)
writeFile('web/vite.config.ts', modifiedContent)
result = runCommand('npm run build', cwd='web/')
assert result.exitCode != 0
# Restore
copyFile('web/vite.config.ts.bak', 'web/vite.config.ts')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when `eslint-plugin-react` or `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` is omitted from the ESLint config, React-specific rule violations go undetected.
edge_case_tests.expected: The scaffold's `eslint.config.js` must reference both plugins; absence of either is a configuration defect.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/eslint.config.js')
assert content.contains('eslint-plugin-react'), 'eslint-plugin-react must be present'
assert content.contains('eslint-plugin-react-hooks'), 'eslint-plugin-react-hooks must be present'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when `tailwind.config.ts` content paths do not cover `src/` files, Tailwind purges all utility classes from the production build.
edge_case_tests.input: `tailwind.config.ts` content paths that do not include `./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`.
edge_case_tests.expected: The scaffold's `tailwind.config.ts` content array must include a glob covering `src/`; any deviation means classes will be purged.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/tailwind.config.ts')
assert content.contains('./src/**/')
assert content.contains('{ts,tsx,js,jsx}') OR content.contains('tsx')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when shadcn/ui CSS variable declarations are absent from `globals.css`, shadcn/ui components render without correct colours.
edge_case_tests.input: `globals.css` missing `:root` CSS variable declarations for `--background`, `--foreground`, etc.
edge_case_tests.expected: The scaffold's `globals.css` must contain CSS variable declarations under `:root` and `.dark`; absence would cause unstyled rendering.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/globals.css')
assert content.contains(':root')
assert content.contains('--background')
assert content.contains('--foreground')
assert content.contains('--primary')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that no path `rewrite` is configured on the `/api`, `/healthz`, or `/readyz` proxy rules in `vite.config.ts`.
edge_case_tests.expected: None of the proxy rule objects for `/api`, `/healthz`, or `/readyz` contain a `rewrite` property.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
# Ensure no rewrite key appears adjacent to any proxy rule
proxySection = extractProxySection(content)
assert NOT proxySection.contains('rewrite')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when Vite is invoked directly (not via `npm run dev`), `__APP_VERSION__` is injected as the string `'undefined'` because `npm_package_version` is not set.
edge_case_tests.input: Vite invoked via `npx vite` directly (not `npm run dev`); `process.env.npm_package_version` is `undefined`.
edge_case_tests.expected: `__APP_VERSION__` is injected as the string `'undefined'`; this is known and documented behaviour.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
# Validate that vite.config.ts uses npm_package_version
content = readFile('web/vite.config.ts')
assert content.contains('process.env.npm_package_version')
# Document known behavior: direct npx vite invocation yields 'undefined'
# Assertion: npm scripts correctly populate npm_package_version
result = runCommandWithEnv('npm run build', cwd='web/', env={'npm_package_version': '1.2.3'})
assert result.exitCode == 0
# Build output contains injected version
buildOutput = readFile('web/dist/assets/index.js') OR firstJSFile('web/dist/')
assert buildOutput.contains('1.2.3')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when the catch-all route is absent from the router configuration, unmatched paths render a blank page or error.
edge_case_tests.expected: The scaffold's `App.tsx` must contain a wildcard catch-all route; its absence means unmatched paths would produce no output.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('web/src/App.tsx')
assert content.contains('path="*"') OR content.contains("path: '*'") OR content.contains('path={"*"}')

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: developer starts the frontend dev server via `make web-dev` and verifies the hello-world page renders at `http://localhost:5173/` with the `af-hub` heading, version string, and shadcn/ui Button.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run `make web-dev` from the repository root; wait for the dev server to bind to port 5173; navigate a headless browser to `http://localhost:5173/`.
mockable: Go backend at `http://localhost:8080` (not required; proxy fails silently)
expected_effects: Vite dev server starts and binds to `http://localhost:5173` without error.
The page at `http://localhost:5173/` renders HTML containing a heading with the text `af-hub`.
The page contains the application version string (from `package.json` via `__APP_VERSION__`).
A shadcn/ui `Button` element is present and visually styled in the rendered DOM.
`QueryClientProvider` and `BrowserRouter` are present in the React component tree.

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: developer runs `make web-build` and verifies a production build is produced in `web/dist/` with bundled assets.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run `make web-build` from the repository root with dependencies installed via `npm ci`.
real_components: `web/src/` all TypeScript and CSS source files
mockable: No components need to be mocked for a build run.
expected_effects: `make web-build` exits with code 0.
Directory `web/dist/` exists and contains at least one HTML file, one JS bundle, and one CSS file.
`__APP_VERSION__` is injected into the JS bundle from `process.env.npm_package_version`.
`@/` alias imports are resolved correctly in the bundle.
Tailwind CSS utility classes used by the scaffold are included in the output CSS.

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: developer runs `make web-lint` and verifies ESLint and TypeScript type checking both pass with exit code 0.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run `make web-lint` from the repository root with dependencies installed.
mockable: No components need to be mocked for a lint run.
expected_effects: `make web-lint` exits with code 0.
ESLint produces zero errors on all files under `web/src/`.
TypeScript compiler produces zero type errors.
No `react/react-in-jsx-scope` errors appear in ESLint output.
Both ESLint and `tsc --noEmit` complete (do not hang or loop indefinitely).

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: a user navigates to an undefined path and sees the 404 catch-all page with a link back to `/`.
smoke_tests.trigger: With the Vite dev server running on port 5173, navigate a headless browser to `http://localhost:5173/nonexistent-page`.
mockable: Go backend at `http://localhost:8080` (not required for this path).
expected_effects: The page at `http://localhost:5173/nonexistent-page` renders the not-found component.
The rendered DOM contains text matching `404` and `Page not found` or similar.
A clickable link to `/` is present in the rendered DOM.
Clicking the link navigates to `http://localhost:5173/` and renders the hello-world page (client-side routing, no full page reload).

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: a browser request to `/api/example` is proxied verbatim to `http://localhost:8080/api/example` by the Vite dev proxy.
smoke_tests.trigger: With the Vite dev server running on port 5173 and the Go backend running on port 8080, make an HTTP GET request to `http://localhost:5173/api/example`.
mockable: Go backend response (a mock HTTP server listening on port 8080 that records incoming request paths).
expected_effects: The Vite dev proxy forwards the request to `http://localhost:8080/api/example` with the path unchanged.
The `Host` header in the forwarded request is rewritten to `localhost:8080` (due to `changeOrigin: true`).
The response from the backend (or mock) is relayed back to the browser.
No path segments are stripped, prepended, or rewritten in the forwarded request.
